document.title: Usability Engineering Report for B Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

sourceSummary.oneLine: Usability engineering report for H&abyz B Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models B1417MCW, B1717MCW, FB1417MCW, and FB1717MCW.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Defines the manufacturer usability engineering process, use specification, user interface risk controls, verification, and summative evaluation references for the device.
keyPoints: The device is intended for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children, and is not to be used for mammography.
Applicable usability standards are EN 62366-1:2015 and EN 60601-1-6:2010.
The report identifies primary operating functions, potential use errors, hazard-related use scenarios, and user interface requirements.
All identified usage scenarios in section 5.4 were selected for summative evaluation.
Usability verification items UI-01 through UI-08 were recorded as Pass.

concepts.name: Digital flat panel detector operating principle
concepts.evidence: The indirect type detector consists of a Scintillator for converting X-rays into visible light and a TFT panel for converting them into data.

evidence.claim: The report is document A-TCF-10-108, revision 0.
evidence.evidenceText: Report No.: A-TCF-10-108 (Rev.0)

evidence.claim: The report date and initial release date are Nov 22, 2024.
evidence.evidenceText: 0 Nov 22, 2024 Initial release

evidence.claim: The document applies to Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models B1417MCW, B1717MCW, FB1417MCW, and FB1717MCW.
evidence.evidenceText: Application: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector (Model name: B1417MCW / B1717MCW / FB1417MCW / FB1717MCW)
evidence.pageTarget: B Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: The device is indicated for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children.
evidence.evidenceText: The B1417MCW/B1717MCW/FB1417MCW/FB1717MCW, Digital Flat Panel X-Ray Detector is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.

evidence.claim: The device is not to be used for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for mammography.

evidence.claim: The intended patient population is adults and children except infants, and excludes pregnant women.
evidence.evidenceText: Age group Adult and Children (except infants) ... Patient group/state except pregnant women

evidence.claim: The intended user experience profile is radiologist.
evidence.evidenceText: Experience Radiologist

evidence.claim: The intended use environment is an X-ray room.
evidence.evidenceText: Place X-ray room

evidence.claim: The applicable standards listed in the document overview are EN 62366-1:2015 and EN 60601-1-6:2010.
evidence.evidenceText: Applicable standards: EN 62366-1:2015 and EN 60601-1-6:2010

evidence.claim: Normative references include IEC TR 62366-2:2016 and EN ISO 14971:2019.
evidence.evidenceText: IEC TR 62366-2:2016 Medical devices — Part 2: Guidance on the application of usability engineering to medical devices EN ISO 14971:2019 Medical Devices – Application of risk management to medical devices

evidence.claim: The usability engineering process assesses and mitigates risks caused by usability problems associated with correct use and use errors in normal use.
evidence.evidenceText: This usability engineering process assesses and mitigates risks caused by usability problems associated with correct use and use errors, i.e., normal use.

evidence.claim: The usability engineering process can identify but does not assess or mitigate risks associated with abnormal use.
evidence.evidenceText: it can be used to identify but does not assess or mitigate risks associated with abnormal use.

evidence.claim: Primary operating functions include checking the user manual, preparing the product, product use, and maintenance.
evidence.evidenceText: Use scenarios related to the primary operating functions 1 Check the User manual 2 Preparation for using the product 3 product use 4 Maintenance

evidence.claim: Foreseeable usability hazards include no reading, inappropriate image result, electric energy, failure of function, and failure of electronic and electric shock.
evidence.evidenceText: hazardous situations hazards ... no reading ... inappropriate image result ... Electric energy ... failure of function ... failure of electronic and electric shock

evidence.claim: The report identifies usability harms including delayed diagnosis of patients, misdiagnosis, electric shock, and burn.
evidence.evidenceText: Harm During Normal Use ... Delayed diagnosis of patients ... misdiagnosis ... Electric shock ... burn

evidence.claim: All usage scenarios identified in section 5.4 were used for summative evaluation.
evidence.evidenceText: All usage scenarios we identified in section 5.4 were used for summative evaluation.

evidence.claim: The summative evaluation report is recorded in Usability Validation Report A-TCF-10-107.
evidence.evidenceText: The summative evaluation report is recorded in Usability Validation Report (Doc. No. A-TCF-10-107).

evidence.claim: User interface requirements include instructions for product use, power-on, operation checks, cleaning, storage, simple use method, output control limitation, and indicators.
evidence.evidenceText: Write user-friendly, easy-to-understand instructions on how to use the product. Add the instructions how to turn on the device ... Add the indicator

evidence.claim: Usability verification items UI-01 through UI-08 passed with no issues.
evidence.evidenceText: Usability Verification Result UI-01 No issues Pass ... UI-08 No issues Pass

evidence.claim: User Interface of Unknown Provenance evaluation is not applicable to this device.
evidence.evidenceText: However, this clause is not applicable on our device, Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector (B1417MCW/B1717MCW/FB1417MCW/FB1717MCW).
